mnemonics: V.I.P. Knapsack: Value Items Profitably using Memory Functions to avoid re-computing the weight of past decisions.
keyPoints: The Knapsack Problem is an optimization problem where you select a subset of items with maximum total value, without exceeding a given weight capacity.

Memory Functions (a top-down approach) enhance naive recursion by storing results of solved subproblems in a table to avoid redundant calculations, combining the clarity of recursion with the efficiency of dynamic programming.
The core recurrence relation for the 0/1 Knapsack is: V[i, w] = max(V[i-1, w], value_i + V[i-1, w - weight_i]) if weight_i <= w, otherwise V[i-1, w].
commonMistakes: Incorrectly initializing the DP table; the first row (no items) should be zeros, and the first column (zero capacity) should be zeros.
Forgetting that the 0/1 Knapsack problem does not allow taking fractional items or multiple copies of the same item, which differentiates it from the fractional knapsack variant.
Off-by-one errors in array indexing when implementing the recurrence relation, especially when translating the theoretical 'i' and 'w' indices to zero-based array indices in code.
